title: Aureline temporary-handoff record, expiry-window, and degraded-state-on-lapse contract
description: Boundary schema for one temporary_handoff_record frozen in docs/governance/service_ownership_card_contract.md §11. The record is the reusable projection that the governance dashboard, the milestone scorecard, the release-evidence shiproom packet, the support bundle, the public claim manifest, the compatibility-window report, and the enterprise / managed-tenant review quote when ownership of a surface is temporarily routed to a substitute owner. Every record names the parent service-ownership card, the primary DRI's role, the temporary owner's role, the start date, the intended expiry, the optional close-out, the typed temporary-handoff status class, the linked evidence packets and waiver records that gate the handoff, the typed closure-or-renewal path, the typed escalation path, and the typed degraded-state label that surfaces MUST render when the handoff lapses. A lapsed handoff can never collapse into a clean active-owner card; an indefinite handoff can never run without a named expiry; a handoff routed under a waiver can never run without the waiver ref. Personal handles, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, raw calendar URLs, raw chat-room URLs, raw on-call rotation entries, and raw private meeting refs MUST NOT appear; the record carries opaque role refs, typed vocabulary, and bounded reviewable summaries only. Adding a new temporary-handoff status class, closure-or-renewal class, or escalation-path class is additive-minor and bumps temporary_handoff_record_schema_version; repurposing an existing value, weakening any allOf invariant, or collapsing two distinct closure paths into one bare label is breaking and requires a new decision row.

temporary_handoff_record_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the temporary-handoff record payload shape. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable identifier safe for live surfaces, support bundles, public-proof packets, claim manifests, release packets, enterprise-review exports, and screenshots. Raw URLs, raw absolute paths, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, raw chat-room URLs, raw calendar URLs, raw on-call rotation entries, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear here.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short reviewable sentence. Personal handles, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, raw calendar URLs, raw chat-room URLs, raw on-call rotation entries, and raw secret material MUST NOT appear.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures. Never part of the runtime record.

temporary_handoff_status_class.description: Closed seven-class temporary-handoff status vocabulary. A handoff whose status cannot be typed denies with temporary_handoff_status_class_unresolved rather than defaulting.

closure_or_renewal_path_class.description: Closed six-class closure-or-renewal-path vocabulary. Names how the handoff exits its temporary state; a card cannot ride an open-ended handoff without one of the typed exits in flight.

escalation_path_class.description: Closed seven-class escalation-path vocabulary. Mirrors /schemas/governance/waiver_expiry_item.schema.json and /schemas/governance/service_ownership_card.schema.json verbatim.

degraded_state_label_class.description: Closed five-class degraded-state-label vocabulary. Mirrors /schemas/governance/service_ownership_card.schema.json verbatim. A handoff cannot be active and degraded simultaneously.

started_at.description: Timestamp at which the handoff opened. Required.

intended_expires_at.description: Declared expiry timestamp. Required. MUST be in the future at started_at; surfaces recompute temporary_handoff_status_class against this value.

closed_at.description: Timestamp at which the handoff closed. Null while temporary_handoff_status_class is one of { handoff_active_inside_window, handoff_nearing_expiry_within_thirty_days, handoff_nearing_expiry_within_seven_days, handoff_active_under_waiver, handoff_expired_past_due }. Non-null when the handoff closes via renewal, return-to-primary, or correction-program retirement.

evaluated_at.description: Timestamp at which the record was projected. Surfaces recompute temporary_handoff_status_class every time the record is rendered.

export_fields.description: Visual / export parity floor. Every consuming surface that renders the handoff MUST render the same primary_owner_role_ref, temporary_owner_role_ref, started_at, intended_expires_at, temporary_handoff_status_class, closure_or_renewal_path_class, escalation_path_class, and degraded_state_label_class. The handoff is intentionally not exported on the public claim manifest; ownership identity at that level routes through the parent ownership card with a redacted role-class export.

temporary_handoff_id.description: Stable, machine-readable id quoted by the parent service-ownership card and by every consuming surface.

service_ownership_card_ref.description: Opaque ref into the parent service_ownership_card_record. Required.

primary_owner_role_ref.description: Role id of the primary DRI the handoff is replacing. Required.

temporary_owner_role_ref.description: Role id of the temporary owner the handoff routes to. Required. MUST NOT equal primary_owner_role_ref; a handoff cannot route to itself.

linked_evidence_refs.description: Refs into the evidence packets that gate the handoff (council decision row, leave-coverage waiver, recused-reviewer decision packet, lab-outage incident packet). At least one entry is required so the handoff can never float free of an upstream packet.

linked_waiver_refs.description: Refs into waivers in artifacts/governance/ownership_matrix.yaml that gate the handoff. Required to be non-empty when temporary_handoff_status_class = handoff_active_under_waiver.

successor_handoff_ref.description: Opaque ref into the successor temporary_handoff_record when the handoff closes via renewal. Required to be non-null when temporary_handoff_status_class = handoff_closed_with_renewal; null otherwise.

decision_forum_ref.description: Opaque ref into the forum id from docs/governance/forum_charters.md the closure / escalation routes through. Required when escalation_path_class is in { escalate_to_decision_forum, escalate_to_release_council, escalate_to_shiproom } or when closure_or_renewal_path_class is in { escalate_to_decision_forum, escalate_to_release_council, escalate_to_shiproom }. Null admissible only when escalation_path_class = no_escalation_required and closure_or_renewal_path_class is in { return_to_primary_dri, renew_handoff_with_named_expiry, retire_via_correction_program }.

handoff_summary.description: One-sentence reviewable summary naming the primary owner role, the temporary owner role, the start date, the intended expiry, and the closure / renewal path. Personal handles, raw email addresses, raw phone numbers, and raw calendar URLs MUST NOT appear.

allOf.description: handoff_active_inside_window, handoff_nearing_expiry_within_thirty_days, handoff_nearing_expiry_within_seven_days, and handoff_active_under_waiver require closed_at to be null.
handoff_expired_past_due requires closed_at to be null and degraded_state_label_class = degraded_handoff_expired so a lapsed handoff cannot collapse into a clean active-owner posture.
handoff_closed_with_renewal requires a non-null closed_at and a non-null successor_handoff_ref so the renewal can be reconstructed.
handoff_closed_returned_to_primary requires a non-null closed_at, a null successor_handoff_ref, and closure_or_renewal_path_class = return_to_primary_dri.
handoff_active_under_waiver requires a non-empty linked_waiver_refs array.
escalation_path_class in { escalate_to_decision_forum, escalate_to_release_council, escalate_to_shiproom } requires a non-null decision_forum_ref.
closure_or_renewal_path_class in { escalate_to_decision_forum, escalate_to_release_council, escalate_to_shiproom } requires a non-null decision_forum_ref so the escalation has a routing target.
Active and near-expiry status classes forbid the handoff from rendering as degraded_handoff_expired; only handoff_expired_past_due may carry that label.
